message:
chore(beads): refine bd→br migration and release notes beads with full specs

bd-3v9s: 17 affected locations, cli_detect.go design, fallback strategy, 28+ tests
bd-37ki: Exact workflow trigger, idempotency marker, tag resolution, 14+ tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-37ki","title":"Add fallback release notes workflow for manual releases","description":"When GoReleaser fails and binaries are uploaded manually through GitHub UI, the release gets no release notes. Need a fallback workflow.

Implementation:
1. Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml triggered on release: [created] events
2. Detect releases with empty or minimal body (< 50 chars)
3. Call GitHub release notes API: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes with tag_name and previous_tag_name
4. Update the release body with generated notes
5. Replicate .goreleaser.yaml changelog filters (exclude docs: and test: prefixes)
6. Test with both automated and manual release scenarios

Reference: .goreleaser.yaml lines 33-38 define current changelog config","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:08:15.092410581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:08:15.092410581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-37ki","title":"Add fallback release notes workflow for manual releases","description":"When GoReleaser fails and binaries are uploaded manually through GitHub UI, the release gets no release notes. Need a fallback workflow that auto-generates notes for any release with minimal/empty body.

Implementation:

1. Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml:
   triggers:
     on:
       release:
         types: [created, edited]
   permissions:
     contents: write

2. Detection logic:
   - Fetch release body via gh api
   - Skip if body length >= 100 chars (already has meaningful notes)
   - Skip if body contains "## " (has markdown headers = intentional content)
   - Skip if body contains idempotency marker "<!-- auto-generated-notes -->"
   - Skip draft releases (only process published releases)

3. Previous tag detection:
   ```bash
   CURRENT_TAG="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
   PREVIOUS_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 --exclude="$CURRENT_TAG" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   if [ -z "$PREVIOUS_TAG" ]; then
     PREVIOUS_TAG=$(git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD | head -1)
   fi
   ```

4. Generate release notes via GitHub API:
   gh api repos/$REPO/releases/generate-notes \
     -f tag_name="$CURRENT_TAG" \
     -f previous_tag_name="$PREVIOUS_TAG" \
     --jq .body

5. Apply changelog filters matching .goreleaser.yaml (lines 33-38):
   - Exclude lines starting with "* docs:" or "* test:" or "* chore:"
   - Preserve all other commit types (feat, fix, refactor, perf, etc.)

6. Append idempotency marker to prevent duplicate runs:
   BODY="$GENERATED_NOTES\n\n<!-- auto-generated-notes -->"

7. Update release:
   gh api repos/$REPO/releases/$RELEASE_ID \
     -X PATCH \
     -f body="$BODY"

8. Error handling:
   - GitHub API rate limit (429): retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts)
   - Permission errors (403): fail with clear message about token permissions
   - Empty generated notes: append "No notable changes in this release." instead of leaving blank
   - Tag format validation: support both v-prefixed (v1.2.3) and bare (1.2.3) tags
   - First release (no previous tag): include all commits from initial commit

EDGE CASES:
- Release body has markdown but < 100 chars: check for "## " header to avoid overwriting intentional content
- Previous tag is ancient (1000+ commits): GitHub API handles pagination, notes may be truncated
- Multiple releases created rapidly: each triggers independently; idempotency marker prevents double-write
- GoReleaser runs AFTER manual release: GoReleaser updates body, next edit event re-triggers but marker prevents overwrite
- Non-semver tag format (release-20260206): git describe still works, API generates notes normally
- Release body has HTML entities: API returns raw markdown, no entity issues

TESTS:

Integration Tests (GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch for manual testing):
- test_manual_release_empty_body: create release with empty body -> notes generated
- test_manual_release_short_body: body = "bugfix" -> notes generated (< 100 chars)
- test_manual_release_long_body: body = 200 chars of real notes -> skipped (has content)
- test_manual_release_with_headers: body = "## Notes\nSomething" -> skipped (has ## header)
- test_idempotency: run workflow twice -> body unchanged on second run (marker detected)
- test_first_release: no previous tag -> all commits included
- test_draft_release: draft release -> skipped (only published)

E2E Tests (run in CI after workflow creation):
- test_goreleaser_then_fallback: GoReleaser succeeds -> fallback skips (body already populated)
- test_goreleaser_fail_then_manual: GoReleaser fails, manual upload -> fallback fills notes
- test_filter_docs_test_commits: verify docs: and test: commits excluded from generated notes
- test_empty_changelog: only docs/test commits between tags -> "No notable changes" message

Manual Verification Checklist:
- [ ] Create manual release via GitHub UI with empty body
- [ ] Verify workflow triggers within 60 seconds
- [ ] Verify release body populated with filtered changelog
- [ ] Verify idempotency marker present in body source
- [ ] Re-trigger workflow -> body unchanged
- [ ] Compare output with GoReleaser-generated notes for consistency

- pkg/analysis/bench_realdata_test.go

Child beads can now proceed with specific optimizations.

 {"id":"bd-3fqy","title":"TOON: Update --help and robot help docs","description":"Document TOON format in CLI help.

## Files
- `cmd/bv/main.go` - Flag help text
- README.md - Usage examples

## Help Text
```
OUTPUT FORMAT
  --format toon    Token-optimized output (~50% fewer tokens than JSON)
  BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT=toon   Environment variable alternative
```

## README Addition
```markdown
### Token-Optimized Output (TOON)

For AI agents, TOON format reduces token usage by ~50%:

\`\`\`bash
bv --robot-triage --format toon
bv --robot-insights --format toon
export BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT=toon
\`\`\`
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:20:37.084755450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:50.264355110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:50.264332086Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - --robot-help includes format documentation, --format flag has help text","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-3v9s","title":"Replace hardcoded bd references with br throughout","description":"bv still emits hardcoded bd (old Go binary) commands in robot output, triage recommendations, markdown exports, and emit-script mode. All should use br (beads_rust).

Affected locations:
- cmd/bv/main.go:2779 — bd update in robot-next claim command
- cmd/bv/main.go:2997 — bd update in triage recommendation comments
- cmd/bv/main.go:3007-3012 — bd update in quick actions
- pkg/analysis/triage.go:970-971 — bd update --status in_progress
- pkg/analysis/triage.go:1571,1631 — ClaimCommand fields

Implementation:
1. Create pkg/analysis/cli_detect.go with BeadsCLI() (auto-detect br vs bd on PATH, prefer br), ClaimCommand(id), ShowCommand(id)
2. Replace all hardcoded bd references with dynamic BeadsCLI() calls
3. Switch from 'bd update --status=in_progress' to 'br update --claim' format (atomic, assigns actor)
4. Update --emit-script 'claim all' loop to use ClaimCommand()
5. Update markdown export commands to use ClaimCommand()
6. Add tests for CLI detection and command generation","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:08:09.417151886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:08:09.417151886Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3v9s","title":"Replace hardcoded bd references with br throughout","description":"bv still emits hardcoded bd (old Go binary) commands in robot output, triage recommendations, markdown exports, and emit-script mode. All should use br (beads_rust). This is a bug since bd is deprecated.

ALL AFFECTED LOCATIONS (17 sites across 4 files):

cmd/bv/main.go:
- Line 2779: robot-next ClaimCmd (bd update %s --status=in_progress)
- Line 2780: robot-next ShowCmd (bd show %s)
- Line 2997: emit-script claim comment
- Line 2999: emit-script show command
- Line 3007: emit-script quick actions (top pick)
- Line 3012: emit-script quick actions (all items)

pkg/analysis/triage.go:
- Line 964: buildCommands() listReady (bd ready)
- Line 965: buildCommands() listBlocked (bd blocked)
- Line 970: buildCommands() claimTop (bd update --status in_progress) [note: missing = sign]
- Line 971: buildCommands() showTop (bd show)
- Line 1571: TrackRecommendationGroup ClaimCommand (CI=1 bd update --status in_progress --json)
- Line 1631: LabelRecommendationGroup ClaimCommand

pkg/export/markdown.go:
- Line 390: bd show bulk command
- Line 396: bd update bulk command
- Lines 422,428,436: bd update/show per-issue
- Line 439: bd show final

pkg/analysis/label_suggest.go:
- Line 242: bd update --add-label suggestion

Implementation:

1. Create pkg/analysis/cli_detect.go:
   - BeadsCLI() string: auto-detect br vs bd on PATH via exec.LookPath(); prefer br; error if neither found
   - ClaimCommand(id string) string: returns "br update ID --claim" (atomic) when br, or "bd update ID --status=in_progress" when bd
   - ShowCommand(id string) string: returns "br show ID" or "bd show ID"
   - ListReady() string: returns "br ready" or "bd ready"
   - ListBlocked() string: returns "br blocked" or "bd blocked"
   - LabelCommand(id, label string) string: for add-label operations
   - Cache detection result in package-level var (detect once, use many times)

2. Replace all 17 hardcoded bd references with dynamic calls to cli_detect functions
3. Unify command format: all robot/CI commands use "CI=1 br update ID --claim --json" pattern
4. Shell-escape IDs in emit-script output (use existing shellEscape helper where present)
5. Update existing tests to validate both br and bd command formats

EDGE CASES:
- Neither br nor bd on PATH: BeadsCLI() returns "br" with warning log (reasonable default since br is current)
- Old br version without --claim flag: ClaimCommand falls back to "br update ID --status=in_progress --actor $USER"
- IDs containing shell special chars: escape with single quotes in emit-script mode
- Windows paths in emit-script: use forward slashes (br handles both)
- Empty recommendations: quick actions section still generates valid (but empty) script

TESTS (28+ required):

Unit Tests (pkg/analysis/cli_detect_test.go):
- test_beads_cli_prefers_br: when both br and bd on PATH, returns "br"
- test_beads_cli_fallback_bd: when only bd on PATH, returns "bd"
- test_beads_cli_neither: when neither on PATH, returns "br" (default)
- test_claim_command_br: format is "br update ID --claim"
- test_claim_command_bd: format is "bd update ID --status=in_progress"
- test_show_command_format: both br and bd variants correct
- test_list_ready_format: both br and bd variants
- test_list_blocked_format: both br and bd variants
- test_label_command_format: both br and bd variants
- test_shell_escape_ids: IDs with spaces, quotes, slashes escaped properly
- test_detection_cached: BeadsCLI() called 100x returns same result without 100 exec.LookPath calls

Integration Tests (existing test files updated):
- test_robot_next_uses_br: --robot-next JSON contains "br" not "bd"
- test_robot_triage_uses_br: --robot-triage commands use "br"
- test_emit_script_uses_br: --emit-script bash output uses "br"
- test_emit_script_claim_all_loop: "claim all" loop uses correct br format
- test_markdown_export_uses_br: markdown export commands use "br"
- test_track_recommendation_claim: TrackRecommendationGroup.ClaimCommand uses "br update --claim"
- test_label_recommendation_claim: LabelRecommendationGroup.ClaimCommand uses "br update --claim"
- test_label_suggest_uses_br: label suggestion actions use "br"

E2E Tests:
- test_robot_next_output_contract: full --robot-next output validates against JSON schema with br commands
- test_emit_script_executable: emitted bash script is valid bash (shellcheck passes)
- test_markdown_export_valid: exported markdown has correct br commands

Regression:
- All existing emit_script_test.go tests updated to accept br instead of bd
- All existing robot_contract_test.go tests updated (lines 473-476)
